sub_mechanisms: M_1: select horizontal frame on parameter manifold
M_2: lift gradient to horizontal subbundle of dimension < ambient
M_3: track vertical commutator as topological defect indicator
M_4: take sub-Riemannian geodesic step preserving horizontality
stripped_llm_application: Adapter updates restricted to a bracket-generating rank-deficient distribution on the parameter manifold; updates lift gradient to a horizontal subbundle, take a geodesic step constrained to this subbundle, and track the commutator (vertical Lie bracket) as a defect indicator. The distribution is non-integrable, so the commutator is non-zero in general.
novelty_claim_stripped: Riemannian-Preconditioned LoRA / GeoLoRA use full-rank Riemannian metrics on low-rank quotient manifolds. None use a rank-deficient bracket-generating distribution with vertical-commutator defect tracking.
